pages.raw_text:
The Great Northern has a diver
at work today at the scene OF the
recent wreck above Camden,
where Superintendent Hood is in
personal charge. One Of the chief
pieces OF. work today is the attempt
to rescue the three bodies known
% be remaining in the lake. The
body of Express Messenger Strick-
land was recovered yesterday.


The chances are that the body OF
Mail Clerk Banner will be recover
ed only when the mail car is got
into such a position as to allow
the opening of the door. The
wrecking gang and crane is work
ing on the baggage car and the
mail car is next in order. Yester-
day some stray pieces Of mail
hoated to the surface. probably
through some aperture in the car
the nature of which has not yet
been found.


If the bodies Of Engineer Munson
and Fireman Bell are beneath the
locomotive, it is not expected that
they will be recovered until the
big machine is moved. 1t is still
a question whether the engine lies
IN such a position that 1t can be
raised. Only the report of the diver
can settle that. In case he is able
to get chain around the big ma
chine, the biggest part Of the
problem will be solved, as the
crane will then be able to haul it
up.
